title: Nanodrop Spectrophotometer (ND-1000) for Nucleic Acid Measurement
author: Unknown
license_note: Confirmed via CrossRef DOI metadata: dx.doi.org/10.17504/protocols.io.id2ca8e
materials: Blank sample (buffer or RNase free H2O)

steps.title: Open ND-1000 V3
steps.instruction: Open ND-1000 V3.8.1 program using the computer connected to the Nanodrop

steps.title: Once the program is opened, click...
steps.instruction: Once the program is opened, click on the corresponding application module (e.g., Nucleic acid to determine the concentration and purity of nucleic acid)

steps.title: Open the sampling arm and load a blank sample (e
steps.instruction: Open the sampling arm and load a blank sample (e.g., buffer or RNase free H 2 O) undetermined

steps.title: Close the arm on the machine...
steps.instruction: Close the arm on the machine to cover the blank sample

steps.title: When the machine is finished, open...
steps.instruction: When the machine is finished, open the sampling arm and clean the blank off the the upper and lower pedestals with a Kimwipe

steps.title: Load your DNA or RNA sample...
steps.instruction: Load your DNA or RNA sample and close the arm undetermined

steps.title: Click on the sample type in...
steps.instruction: Click on the sample type in the upper right-hand corner of the program (e.g., DNA or RNA)

steps.title: Click "Measure" in the upper left...
steps.instruction: Click "Measure" in the upper left hand corner of the program

steps.title: Once the machine is finished reading...
steps.instruction: Once the machine is finished reading the sample, read the 260/280, 260/230 and concentration (ng/µL) measurements.

steps.title: Clean the nucleic acid sample off...
steps.instruction: Clean the nucleic acid sample off of the upper and lower pedestals with a Kimwipe
